workflow_stages.name: scope, authority, and compute context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm compute scope, request intent, desired artifact, provider, region, workload owner, business outcome, environment, urgency, source precedence, and adjacent-lane indicators before producing guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: tenant evidence and system-of-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect workload, compute inventory, autoscaling, quota, topology, image, patch, backup, monitoring, cost, incident, change, IaC, policy, owner, and exception records from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: source selection and precedence review
workflow_stages.description: Choose current provider, Kubernetes, Terraform, NIST, CIS, and tenant sources; record freshness, scope, conflicts, and whether tenant or contractual policy overrides public baseline guidance.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: compute option and tradeoff analysis
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate sizing, placement, autoscaling, quota, failure-domain, patch, backup, restore, DR, rollback, cost, isolation, capacity reservation, and operability options against tenant constraints and failure modes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: operations, troubleshooting, and lifecycle guidance
workflow_stages.description: Prepare triage order, evidence collection, containment, patching, image rollout, node replacement, restore test, DR drill, rollback, and owner handoff guidance without executing tenant changes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: artifact delivery, refusal, or orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Deliver an evidence-linked compute architecture recommendation, decision log, runbook, implementation guardrail, or explicit refusal and escalation note with confidence state and unresolved assumptions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: implementation guardrails for server lifecycle operations
VM, scale-set, managed instance group, Auto Scaling group, and node pool sizing matrix
region, zone, placement, failure-domain, and isolation recommendation
autoscaling, quota, capacity reservation, spot or preemptible, and commitment tradeoff matrix
backup, restore-test, DR drill, failover/failback, and rollback plan
Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM/Bicep, Deployment Manager, or Kubernetes node IaC review notes
boundary refusal, escalation, or adjacent-lane handoff note
systems_of_record: AWS EC2, EC2 Auto Scaling, launch templates, placement groups, Systems Manager Patch Manager, CloudWatch, CloudTrail, AWS Config, AWS Backup, Compute Optimizer, Cost Explorer, Service Quotas, Trusted Advisor, Health Dashboard, CloudFormation, and Terraform state

Azure Virtual Machines, Virtual Machine Scale Sets, availability sets, Azure Resource Graph, Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, VM insights, Azure Backup, Azure Policy, Update Manager, Cost Management, service health, ARM or Bicep templates, and Terraform state

Google Compute Engine instances, machine images, managed instance groups, instance templates, operations logs, Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Cloud Audit Logs, Backup and DR, quotas, recommender outputs, billing export, Deployment Manager if present, and Terraform state
Kubernetes API server, node objects, node conditions, events, cluster autoscaler or managed autoscaling records, maintenance and upgrade records, logs, metrics, and backup or restore records

CMDB, asset inventory, architecture diagrams, ADRs, runbook repositories, incident systems, change-management systems, source repositories, CI/CD systems, policy repositories, evidence stores, and tenant exception registers

allowed: Recommend cloud VM, scale set, managed instance group, Auto Scaling group, node pool, image, patch, autoscaling, capacity, quota, placement, resilience, recovery, and compute IaC options.
Prepare implementation guidance, validation checkpoints, rollback moments, runbooks, evidence requests, and owner handoffs.
Identify compute infrastructure risks, tradeoffs, assumptions, and domain failure modes without approving execution, spend, risk acceptance, or legal/compliance claims.
escalate: Required workload facts, provider records, telemetry, backup evidence, quota posture, source freshness, source precedence, data classification, RTO/RPO, SLO, region, zone, owner authority, or change approval is missing.
Provider documentation, tenant policy, contractual requirements, customer commitments, regulatory obligations, or reviewer instructions conflict.

The request needs architecture review board, change advisory board, incident command, provider support, security, identity, network, database, backup, FinOps, legal, compliance, procurement, or executive approval.
refuse: Application-only engineering, database schema design, network deep design, endpoint or IAM ownership, security investigation, managed security product tuning, help-desk intake, legal advice, procurement approval, compliance attestation, production execution, or risk acceptance.

Requests to execute infrastructure changes, bypass change control, perform failover, restore production systems, apply IaC, alter accounts, handle secrets, approve spend, or direct provider support work.
Requests to treat generic cloud advice as compute infrastructure guidance without provider-specific records, artifacts, systems, and decision boundaries.

domain_failure_modes: Generic cloud guidance ignores provider-specific VM family, quota, region, zone, maintenance, service-limit, host, image, disk, network attachment, or autoscaling behavior.
Sizing recommendations omit workload demand shape, statefulness, latency, IO, growth, dependency, SLO, RTO/RPO, or validation evidence.
Placement recommendations collapse Availability Zone, region, availability set, placement group, managed instance group, node pool, tenancy, and failure-domain semantics.
Autoscaling guidance ignores health checks, warmup, cooldown, lifecycle hooks, launch template or instance template drift, minimum capacity, quota, and rollback implications.
Cost guidance becomes purchase approval or budget decision rather than technical cost-performance evidence and FinOps handoff.
Patch or image refresh guidance ignores canaries, drain behavior, maintenance windows, health evidence, rollback, and application-owner validation.
Backup and recovery guidance recommends RTO/RPO or DR posture without restore-test evidence, ownership, recovery points, dependency order, or failover/failback authority.
IaC review overlooks Terraform state, drift, replacement behavior, lifecycle arguments, plan evidence, rollback, blast radius, and change-control records.
Kubernetes node incidents absorb cluster platform, CNI, admission, or service mesh ownership instead of routing when platform decisions dominate.
Cloud networking, identity, endpoint, security, database, storage, help desk, legal, compliance, or production execution work is absorbed instead of refused or routed.
Public source guidance and tenant policy conflict without precedence resolution.
Cross-industry evidence appears complete but lacks named compute systems of record, owner authority, or provider-specific artifacts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EC2, EC2 Auto Scaling, Azure VMs, VM Scale Sets, Google Compute Engine, managed instance groups, Kubernetes nodes, provider monitoring and logging systems, backup and restore systems, provider quota and health records, Terraform state, cloud-native deployment templates, CI/CD logs, incident tickets, runbooks, change records, cost records, CMDB, diagrams, ADRs, policies, exception registers, and owner approval records govern the lane.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Compute infrastructure fails materially when provider-specific machine families, region and zone semantics, quotas, health checks, host maintenance, autoscaling lifecycle behavior, node pressure, restore evidence, IaC state, capacity commitments, and ownership boundaries are absent; generic cloud advice can create wrong availability, cost, isolation, recovery, or execution assumptions.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Application engineering, database platforms, cloud networking, Kubernetes platform operations, storage and backup platforms, observability ownership, SRE incident command, cloud security, identity and endpoint operations, FinOps approval, procurement, legal, compliance attestation, provider support ownership, production execution, and executive risk acceptance constrain the lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for the spec pack. At runtime, conflicting provider guidance, tenant policy, contractual obligations, regulatory requirements, incident commander instructions, or reviewer decisions require precedence resolution before deterministic guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: provider availability, quota, pricing, commitments, and preview features
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for the spec pack. Runtime materialization must refresh provider region support, zone availability, quotas, instance-family availability, interruption posture, pricing, commitment terms, and preview-feature status when those facts affect the answer.

This package remains specification-based. Retaining a trusted generated implementation requires separate frequent-use evidence, source refresh, tenant isolation review, regression validation, and human approval.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because tenant-specific uncertainty is represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.

The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling using official AWS, Microsoft, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, HashiCorp, NIST, and CIS sources plus the current package baseline only for regression preservation.
The research output constrains workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and scenario families in the refreshed package.
